WITN04630100
WITN04630100

49. Regarding FUJ00084797 (Emails: Local Suspense Problem), I cannot recall what the local suspense problem was, what steps I may have taken nor any views on how Fujitsu dealt with it.

50. Regarding FUJ00081880 (Peak Incident Management System) and POL00029908 (Emails: Dalmellington Outreach Service-horizon transaction discrepancies) again I cannot recall this specific incident, however, the emails indicate that I would have been alerted on 20 October 2015 and I have no reason to doubt that.

51. I agree with Anne Allaker that Fujitsu should have provided an explanation for the four incidents. Within the email extracts there only appears to be detailed narrative about events in the branch for the one named branch, whereas the other four are referenced but with little content. The emails do, however, suggest that a common approach was used to resolve the records in three branches and that it may work for the fourth one.

52. With the passage of time I cannot say whether I would have felt this typical for Fujitsu

53. Finally on that particular issue, my feeling today is that Kendra Dickinson was right to be concerned about giving advice where the underlying circumstances were unclear. However, in hindsight, I wonder whether a file note could have been agreed with FSC and Cash Supply Chain to note the situation on the known branches, to script a process for tracking any further cases and to seek to agree a process and advice ahead of the branches next balancing period. It would therefore perhaps have been appropriate to log calls with all the affected branches with a view to explain that to them,

Page 13 of 24
